message:
test(e2e-large,bd-3ii77): mark all 5 large-dataset e2e tests #[ignore] for opt-in long-run gate

Pre-fix: `tests/e2e_large_dataset.rs` ran 5 large-dataset tests
unconditionally on every `cargo test --all-targets` invocation.
`index_large_single_session` alone could take several minutes
(2500 message pairs × index+search subprocess pipeline), so the
routine green gate either timed out or interactively stalled,
blocking the 3e3qg.6 / 3ii77 deterministic-suite closure. The f2r5t
timeout wrapper (commit f752ae40 / 2gypv retrofit) already converted
silent hangs into diagnostic panics, but the per-test budgets
(300s index / 30s search × 5 tests) are fine for an opt-in run and
far too heavy for the routine gate.

Per bead 3ii77's second-clause closure policy ("explicitly moved
out of the routine all-targets gate with a documented CI shard /
ignore policy and a separate scheduled / long-run gate"), all 5
tests are now `#[ignore = "bd-3ii77: long-run e2e; opt-in via
cargo test -- --ignored"]`. The module-level doc-comment documents
the policy and the explicit opt-in invocation:

    cargo test --test e2e_large_dataset -- --ignored --nocapture

Validation: `cargo test --test e2e_large_dataset` now completes in
0.31s (55 passed, 5 ignored) vs the pre-fix multi-minute stall.

Closes coding_agent_session_search-3ii77.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
